item.title: New 'CoPhish' technique wraps OAuth phishing in Microsoft Copilot
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: A new phishing technique dubbed 'CoPhish' weaponizes Microsoft Copilot Studio agents to deliver fraudulent OAuth consent requests via legitimate and trusted Microsoft domains. [...]

item.title: New CoPhish attack steals OAuth tokens via Copilot Studio agents
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: A new phishing technique dubbed 'CoPhish' weaponizes Microsoft Copilot Studio agents to deliver fraudulent OAuth consent requests via legitimate and trusted Microsoft domains. [...]

item.title: Satellites are LEAKING Your Unencrypted Data
item.author: Seytonic
item.content:encoded:
Get 20% off DeleteMe US consumer plans when you go to https://joindeleteme.com/seytonic and use promo code SEYTONIC at checkout. DeleteMe International Plans: https://international.joindeleteme.com/
0:00 Intro 0:17 Satellites are LEAKING Your Data 3:56 DeleteMe (ad) 5:10 When Hackers Target Kids 7:22 Is Your Mouse Evil?
Sources: https://satcom.sysnet.ucsd.edu/
https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cpvlgzk0xvpo https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c8rv83mrlyyo https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c62ldyvpwv9o

item.title: 'Achtduizend Windows WSUS-servers direct toegankelijk vanaf internet'
item.content:encoded: Meer dan achtduizend Windows WSUS-servers zijn direct toegankelijk vanaf het internet, terwijl dat eigenlijk niet zou moeten. Daardoor lopen deze machines het risico via een kritiek beveiligingslek te ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: CRITICAL ALERT: Windows Server WSUS Flaw Actively Exploited (CVE-2025-59287, CVSS 9.8)
item.content:encoded: Microsoft has recently issued an emergency security update for enterprise Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) to address a critical vulnerability, CVE-2025-59287, which carries a CVSS score of 9.8. ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Apple Loses Landmark UK Antitrust Lawsuit Over ‘Excessive’ App Store Fees
item.content:encoded: According to a report by the Financial Times, Apple has lost a major antitrust class-action lawsuit in the United Kingdom concerning its App Store practices. The Competition Appeal Tribunal (CAT) rule ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: OpenAI Buys Mac Automation App to Give ChatGPT System-Level Control
item.content:encoded: OpenAI recently announced the acquisition of a company called Software Applications, best known for its newly released Mac automation app, Sky, which debuted earlier this year. The pedigree of Softwar ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Cloud Gaming for Free: Amazon Luna Relaunches as a Free Prime Perk
item.content:encoded: Amazon has officially relaunched its cloud gaming and streaming service, Amazon Luna, which had been teased for a major overhaul earlier in October. The revamped platform not only introduces a complet ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Critical WordPress RCE Flaws Resurface: Over 8.7 Million Attacks Exploit GutenKit & Hunk Companion
item.content:encoded: Critical WordPress RCE Flaws Resurface: Over 8.7 Million Attacks Exploit GutenKit & Hunk Companion

item.title: CISA Warns of Hackers Actively Exploiting Windows Server Update Services RCE Vulnerability in the Wild
item.content:encoded: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has warned organizations worldwide about active exploitation of a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in Microsoft’s Win ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Everybody's warning about critical Windows Server WSUS bug exploits ... but Microsoft's mum
item.content:encoded: Governments and private security sleuths warned that attackers are already exploiting a critical bug in Microsoft Windows Server Update Services, shortly after Redmond pushed an emergency patch for th ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Friday Squid Blogging: “El Pulpo The Squid”
item.author: Bruce Schneier
item.content:encoded: There is a new cigar named “ The Squid.” Yes, that means “The Octopus The Squid.”As usual, you can also use this squid post to talk about the security stories in the news that I haven’t covered.

item.title: Hackers launch mass attacks exploiting outdated WordPress plugins
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: A widespread exploitation campaign is targeting WordPress websites with GutenKit and Hunk Companion plugins vulnerable to critical-severity, old security issues that can be used to achieve remote code execution (RCE). [...]

item.title: Smishing Triad Linked to 194,000 Malicious Domains in Global Phishing Operation
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: The threat actors behind a large-scale, ongoing smishing campaign have been attributed to more than 194,000 malicious domains since January 1, 2024, targeting a broad range of services across the world, according to new findings from Palo Alto Networks Unit 42. "Although these domains are registered through a Hong Kong-based registrar and use Chinese nameservers, the attack infrastructure is

item.title: Warlock Ransomware Actors Exploiting Sharepoint ToolShell Zero-Day Vulnerability in New Attack Wave
item.content:encoded: Warlock Ransomware Actors Exploiting Sharepoint ToolShell Zero-Day Vulnerability in New Attack Wave The cybersecurity landscape experienced a significant shift in July 2025 when threat actors associated with Warlock ransomware began exploiting a critical zero-day vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoin ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Critical WSUS Vulnerability RCE CVE-2025-59287
item.content:encoded: October 24, 2025Microsoft’s Windows Server Update Services (WSUS) recently faced a critical security threat that has sent shockwaves through IT security communities worldwide. The vulnerability, track ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Newly Patched Critical Microsoft WSUS Flaw Comes Under Active Exploitation
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Microsoft on Thursday released out-of-band security updates to patch a critical-severity Windows Server Update Service (WSUS) vulnerability with a proof-of-concept (Poc) exploit publicly available and has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2025-59287 (CVSS score: 9.8), a remote code execution flaw in WSUS that was originally fixed by the tech giant

item.title: Critical WSUS flaw in Windows Server now exploited in attacks
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Attackers are now exploiting a critical-severity Windows Server Update Service (WSUS) vulnerability, which already has publicly available proof-of-concept exploit code. [...]

item.title: Amazon: This week’s AWS outage caused by major DNS failure
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Amazon says a major DNS failure was behind a massive AWS (Amazon Web Services) outage that took down many websites and online services on Monday. [...]

item.title: October 2025 Patch Tuesday: Two Publicly Disclosed, Three Zero-Days, and Eight Critical Vulnerabilities Among 172 CVEs
item.content:encoded: October 2025 Patch Tuesday: Two Publicly Disclosed, Three Zero-Days, and Eight Critical Vulnerabilities Among 172 CVEs

item.title: Fake LastPass death claims used to breach password vaults
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: LastPass is warning customers of a phishing campaign sending emails with an access request to the password vault as part of a legacy inheritance process. [...]

item.title: How to reduce costs with self-service password resets
item.author: Sponsored by Specops Software
item.content:encoded: Password resets account for nearly 40% of IT help desk calls, costing orgs time and money. Specops Software's uReset lets users securely reset passwords with flexible MFA options like Duo, Okta, and Yubikey while enforcing identity verification to stop misuse. [...]

item.title: APT36 Targets Indian Government with Golang-Based DeskRAT Malware Campaign
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: A Pakistan-nexus threat actor has been observed targeting Indian government entities as part of spear-phishing attacks designed to deliver a Golang-based malware known as DeskRAT. The activity, observed in August and September 2025 by Sekoia, has been attributed to Transparent Tribe (aka APT36), a state-sponsored hacking group known to be active since at least 2013. It also builds upon a prior

item.title: NCSC waarschuwt voor actief misbruik van kritiek Windows Server-lek
item.content:encoded: Het Nationaal Cyber Security Centrum (NCSC) waarschuwt voor actief misbruik van een kritieke kwetsbaarheid in Windows Server waarvoor Microsoft gisteren een noodpatch uitbracht. Via het beveiligingsle ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Mozilla: New Firefox extensions must disclose data collection practices
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Starting next month, Mozilla will require Firefox extension developers to disclose whether their add-ons collect or share user data with third parties. [...]

item.title: Microsoft drops surprise Windows Server patch before weekend downtime
item.content:encoded: Microsoft has released an out-of-band update to patch a critical vulnerability in Windows Server Update Services (WSUS). The update addresses CVE-2025-59287">CVE-2025-59287, a remote code execution fl ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Microsoft releases urgent fix for actively exploited WSUS vulnerability (CVE-2025-59287)
item.content:encoded: Microsoft releases urgent fix for actively exploited WSUS vulnerability (CVE-2025-59287)

item.title: Microsoft Issues Emergency Patch for Critical WSUS Remote Code Execution Flaw (CVE-2025-59287)
item.content:encoded: Microsoft Issues Emergency Patch for Critical WSUS Remote Code Execution Flaw (CVE-2025-59287)

item.title: Part Four of The Kryptos Sculpture
item.author: Bruce Schneier
item.content:encoded: Two people found the solution. They used the power of research, not cryptanalysis, finding clues amongst the Sanborn papers at the Smithsonian’s Archives of American Art.This comes as an awkward time, as Sanborn is auctioning off the solution. There were legal threats—I don’t understand their basis—and the solvers are not publishing their solution.

item.title: The Cybersecurity Perception Gap: Why Executives and Practitioners See Risk Differently
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Does your organization suffer from a cybersecurity perception gap? Findings from the Bitdefender 2025 Cybersecurity Assessment suggest the answer is probably “yes” — and many leaders may not even realize it. This disconnect matters. Small differences in perception today can evolve into major blind spots tomorrow. After all, perception influences what organizations prioritize, where they

item.title: Vulnerability in Studio Fabryka DobryCMS software
item.content:encoded: Vulnerability in Studio Fabryka DobryCMS software Vulnerability in Studio Fabryka DobryCMS software CVE ID CVE-2025-8536 Publication date 24 October 2025 Vendor Studio Fabryka Product DobryCMS Vulnerable versions 1.x and 2.x Vulnerability type (CWE) ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Microsoft blocks risky file previews in Windows File Explorer
item.content:encoded: Along with fixing many code-based vulnerabilities, the October 2025 Windows updates also change how File Explorer handles files downloaded from the internet. The change affects the file management too ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: 3,000 YouTube Videos Exposed as Malware Traps in Massive Ghost Network Operation
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: A malicious network of YouTube accounts has been observed publishing and promoting videos that lead to malware downloads, essentially abusing the popularity and trust associated with the video hosting platform for propagating malicious payloads. Active since 2021, the network has published more than 3,000 malicious videos to date, with the volume of such videos tripling since the start of the

item.title: Microsoft komt met noodpatch voor kritiek lek in Windows Server
item.content:encoded: Microsoft heeft een noodpatch uitgebracht om een kritieke kwetsbaarheid in Windows Server "volledig" te patchen. Proof-of-concept exploitcode is inmiddels op internet beschikbaar en Microsoft verwacht ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: SessionReaper Exploits Erupt as Magento Sites Lag on Patching
item.content:encoded: Six weeks after Adobe shipped an emergency fix, attackers have begun weaponizing SessionReaper — and most Magento stores still stand exposed. Security firm Sansec’s forensics team said it blocked hund ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Microsoft Releases Emergency Patch For Windows Server Update Service RCE Vulnerability
item.content:encoded: Microsoft has rolled out an out-of-band emergency patch for a remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability affecting the Windows Server Update Services (WSUS). Identified as CVE-2025-59287, the issue ste ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Windows Server emergency patches fix WSUS bug with PoC exploit
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Microsoft has released out-of-band (OOB) security updates to patch a critical-severity Windows Server Update Service (WSUS) vulnerability with publicly available proof-of-concept exploit code. [...]

item.title: WordPress-sites aangevallen via kritiek beveiligingslek in plug-ins
item.content:encoded: WordPress-sites zijn het doelwit van een aanval waarbij misbruik wordt gemaakt van kritieke kwetsbaarheden in twee plug-ins genaamd GutenKit en Hunk Companion. Via de beveiligingslekken kan een ongeau ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Self-Spreading 'GlassWorm' Infects VS Code Extensions in Widespread Supply Chain Attack
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a self-propagating worm that spreads via Visual Studio Code (VS Code) extensions on the Open VSX Registry and the Microsoft Extension Marketplace, underscoring how developers have become a prime target for attacks. The sophisticated threat, codenamed GlassWorm by Koi Security, is the second such supply chain attack to hit the DevOps space within a span

item.title: Vulnerability in Request Tracker software
item.content:encoded: Vulnerability in Request Tracker software CVE ID CVE-2025-9158 Publication date 24 October 2025 Vendor Best Practical Product Request Tracker Vulnerable versions From 5.0.4 through 5.0.8 and from 6.0. ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Hackers earn $1,024,750 for 73 zero-days at Pwn2Own Ireland
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: ​The Pwn2Own Ireland 2025 hacking competition has ended with security researchers collecting $1,024,750 in cash awards after exploiting 73 zero-day vulnerabilities. [...]

item.content:encoded: Warlock Ransomware Hits US Firms Exploiting SharePoint Zero-Day, Linked to China’s CamoFei APT

item.content:encoded: PhantomCaptcha Spyware Targets Ukraine NGOs with Fake Cloudflare Lure to Deploy WebSocket RAT Researchers from SentinelLABS, in collaboration with the Digital Security Lab of Ukraine, have exposed a coordinated spearphishing campaign aimed at humanitarian organizations and regional government ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Toys “R” Us Canada warns customers' info leaked in data breach
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: Toys "R" Us Canada has sent notices of a data breach to customers informing them of a security incident where threat actors leaked customer records they had previously stolen from its systems. [...]

item.title: HP pulls update that broke Microsoft Entra ID auth on some AI PCs
item.author: Lawrence Abrams
item.content:encoded: HP has pulled an HP OneAgent software update for Windows 11 that mistakenly deleted Microsoft certificates required for some organizations to log in to Microsoft Entra ID, effectively disconnecting them from their company's cloud environments. [...]

item.title: Medusa Ransomware Leaks 834 GB of Comcast Data After $1.2M Demand
item.content:encoded: The Medusa ransomware group has leaked 186.36 GB of compressed data it claimed to have stolen from Comcast Corporation, a global media and technology company. According to Hackread.com’s earlier repor ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Norks droning on about your dream job while pwning your PC
item.content:encoded: North Korea's Lazarus Group has successfully compromised Europe's unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) sector with its Operation DreamJob campaign, which promises job seekers lucrative employment opportuniti ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Meet the new Clippy: Microsoft unveils Copilot's "Mico" avatar
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Today, Microsoft introduced Mico, a new and more personal avatar for the AI-powered Copilot digital assistant, which the company describes as human-centered. [...]

item.title: CISA warns of Lanscope Endpoint Manager flaw exploited in attacks
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: The Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is warning that hackers are exploiting a critical vulnerability in the Motex Landscope Endpoint Manager. [...]

item.title: Microsoft disables File Explorer preview for downloads to block attacks
item.author: Sergiu Gatlan
item.content:encoded: Microsoft says that the File Explorer (formerly Windows Explorer) now automatically blocks previews for files downloaded from the Internet to block credential theft attacks via malicious documents. [...]

item.title: North Korean Hackers Lure Defense Engineers With Fake Jobs to Steal Drone Secrets
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Threat actors with ties to North Korea have been attributed to a new wave of attacks targeting European companies active in the defense industry as part of a long-running campaign known as Operation Dream Job. "Some of these [companies] are heavily involved in the unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) sector, suggesting that the operation may be linked to North Korea's current efforts to scale up its

item.title: Microsoft suggests temporary registry hack for stricken smart card users
item.content:encoded: Microsoft accidentally broke several things in the October 2025 Windows Update, but smart card authentication was not one of them. That was intentionally broken, and the temporary workaround requires ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Zero Trust Has a Blind Spot—Your AI Agents
item.author: Sponsored by Token Security
item.content:encoded: AI agents now act, decide, and access systems on their own — creating new blind spots Zero Trust can't see. Token Security helps organizations govern AI identities so every agent's access, intent, and action are verified and accountable. [...]

item.title: Spoofed AI sidebars can trick Atlas, Comet users into dangerous actions
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: OpenAI's Atlas and Perplexity's Comet browsers are vulnerable to AI sidebar spoofing attacks that mislead users into following fake AI-generated instructions. [...]

item.content:encoded: CVE-2025-61932, an “improper verification of source of a communication channel” vulnerability affecting Lanscope Endpoint Manager, has been exploited as a zero-day since April 2025, the Japan Computer ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: North Korean Lazarus hackers targeted European defense companies
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: North Korean Lazarus hackers compromised three European companies in the defense sector through a coordinated Operation DreamJob campaign leveraging fake recruitment lures. [...]

item.title: Hackers Exploiting Adobe Magento RCE Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild – 3 in 5 Stores Vulnerable
item.content:encoded: Hackers Exploiting Adobe Magento RCE Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild – 3 in 5 Stores Vulnerable Hackers have begun actively targeting a critical remote code execution flaw in Adobe’s Magento e-commerce platform, putting thousands of online stores at immediate risk just six weeks after Adobe issu ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Secure AI at Scale and Speed — Learn the Framework in this Free Webinar
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: AI is everywhere—and your company wants in. Faster products, smarter systems, fewer bottlenecks. But if you're in security, that excitement often comes with a sinking feeling. Because while everyone else is racing ahead, you're left trying to manage a growing web of AI agents you didn’t create, can’t fully see, and weren’t designed to control. Join our upcoming webinar and learn how to make AI

item.title: CISA Warns of Motex LANSCOPE Endpoint Manager Vulnerability Exploited in Attacks
item.content:encoded: CISA has issued a critical alert regarding a severe vulnerability in Motex LANSCOPE Endpoint Manager, a popular tool for managing IT assets across networks. Dubbed an improper verification of the sour ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: RCE Vulnerability (CVE-2025-62518) Discovered in Popular Rust Library async-tar and Its Forks
item.content:encoded: RCE Vulnerability (CVE-2025-62518) Discovered in Popular Rust Library async-tar and Its Forks

item.title: ThreatsDay Bulletin: $176M Crypto Fine, Hacking Formula 1, Chromium Vulns, AI Hijack & More
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Criminals don’t need to be clever all the time; they just follow the easiest path in: trick users, exploit stale components, or abuse trusted systems like OAuth and package registries. If your stack or habits make any of those easy, you’re already a target. This week’s ThreatsDay highlights show exactly how those weak points are being exploited — from overlooked

item.title: Critical Adobe Commerce, Magento vulnerability under attack (CVE-2025-54236)
item.content:encoded: Attackers are trying to exploit CVE-2025-54236, a critical vulnerability affecting Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source, Sansec researchers have warned. The company blocked over 250 exploitation att ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Serious F5 Breach
item.author: Bruce Schneier
item.content:encoded: F5, a Seattle-based maker of networking software, disclosed the breach on Wednesday. F5 said a “sophisticated” threat group working for an undisclosed nation-state government had surreptitiously and persistently dwelled in its network over a “long-term.” Security researchers who have responded to similar intrusions in the past took the language to mean the hackers were inside the F5 network for years.During that time, F5 said, the hackers took control of the network segment the company uses to create and distribute updates for BIG IP, a line of server appliances that F5 says is used by 48 of the world’s top 50 corporations. Wednesday’s disclosure went on to say the threat group downloaded proprietary BIG-IP source code information about vulnerabilities that had been privately discovered but not yet patched. The hackers also obtained configuration settings that some customers used inside their networks.Control of the build system and access to the source code, customer configurations, and documentation of unpatched vulnerabilities has the potential to give the hackers unprecedented knowledge of weaknesses and the ability to exploit them in supply-chain attacks on thousands of networks, many of which are sensitive. The theft of customer configurations and other data further raises the risk that sensitive credentials can be abused, F5 and outside security experts said.

item.title: Why Organizations Are Abandoning Static Secrets for Managed Identities
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: As machine identities explode across cloud environments, enterprises report dramatic productivity gains from eliminating static credentials. And only legacy systems remain the weak link. For decades, organizations have relied on static secrets, such as API keys, passwords, and tokens, as unique identifiers for workloads. While this approach provides clear traceability, it creates what security

Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: From Domain User to SYSTEM: Analyzing the NTLM LDAP Authentication Bypass Vulnerability (CVE-2025-54918)
item.content:encoded: From Domain User to SYSTEM: Analyzing the NTLM LDAP Authentication Bypass Vulnerability (CVE-2025-54918) In September 2025, a critical vulnerability (CVE-2025-54918) was discovered affecting domain controllers running LDAP or LDAPS services. This vulnerability allows attackers to elevate privileges from ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: TARmageddon Vulnerability In Rust Library Let Attackers Replace Config Files And Execute Remote Codes
item.content:encoded: A severe vulnerability in the async-tar Rust library and its popular forks, including the widely used tokio-tar. Dubbed TARmageddon and tracked as CVE-2025-62518, the bug carries a CVSS score of 8.1, ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: 'Jingle Thief' Hackers Exploit Cloud Infrastructure to Steal Millions in Gift Cards
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: Cybersecurity researchers have shed light on a cybercriminal group called Jingle Thief that has been observed targeting cloud environments associated with organizations in the retail and consumer services sectors for gift card fraud. "Jingle Thief attackers use phishing and smishing to steal credentials, to compromise organizations that issue gift cards," Palo Alto Networks Unit 42 researchers

item.title: Multiple Oracle VM VirtualBox Vulnerabilities Enables Complete Takeover Of VirtualBox
item.content:encoded: Multiple Oracle VM VirtualBox Vulnerabilities Enables Complete Takeover Of VirtualBox

item.title: Adobe waarschuwt webwinkels voor actief misbruik van kritiek beveiligingslek
item.content:encoded: Adobe waarschuwt webwinkels die gebruikmaken van Adobe Commerce en Magento Open Source voor actief misbruik van een kritieke kwetsbaarheid. Via het beveiligingslek kunnen aanvallers volledige controle ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Multiple BIND 9 DNS Vulnerabilities Enable Cache Poisoning and Denial of Service Attacks
item.content:encoded: The Internet Systems Consortium (ISC) disclosed three high-severity vulnerabilities in BIND 9 on October 22, 2025, potentially allowing remote attackers to conduct cache poisoning attacks or cause den ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Over 250 Magento Stores Hit Overnight as Hackers Exploit New Adobe Commerce Flaw
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: E-commerce security company Sansec has warned that threat actors have begun to exploit a recently disclosed security vulnerability in Adobe Commerce and Magento Open Source platforms, with more than 250 attack attempts recorded against multiple stores over the past 24 hours. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2025-54236 (CVSS score: 9.1), a critical improper input validation flaw that could be

item.title: Critical Lanscope Endpoint Manager Bug Exploited in Ongoing Cyberattacks, CISA Confirms
item.author: The Hacker News
item.content:encoded: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Wednesday added a critical security flaw impacting Motex Lanscope Endpoint Manager to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, stating it has been actively exploited in the wild. The vulnerability, CVE-2025-61932 (CVSS v4 score: 9.3), impacts on-premises versions of Lanscope Endpoint Manager, specifically Client

item.title: Security First: Windows 11 Disables File Previews for Web Downloads
item.content:encoded: Earlier this month, Microsoft released a Windows 11 cumulative update addressing several known feature issues and security vulnerabilities. Among the modifications introduced, one notable change invol ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.content:encoded: Full-Scale War: Apple Challenges EU’s Digital Markets Act in Court The fierce standoff between Apple and the European Union over the Digital Markets Act (DMA) has officially escalated from a war of words into a full-scale legal battle before the EU General Court in L ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.content:encoded: The SUSE Rancher Security team has issued a critical advisory addressing a command injection and buffer overflow vulnerability in NeuVector, the company’s full lifecycle container security platform. T ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Major Threat: Vidar Stealer v2.0 Bypasses Chrome AppBound Encryption with Multithreaded Memory Injection
item.content:encoded: Researchers at Trend Micro have released an in-depth analysis of Vidar Stealer v2.0, a major overhaul of the well-known Vidar information-stealing malware that has resurfaced with powerful new capabil ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: CISA Warns: Critical Raisecom Router Flaw (CVE-2025-11534, CVSS 9.8) Allows Unauthenticated Root SSH Access
item.content:encoded: CISA Warns: Critical Raisecom Router Flaw (CVE-2025-11534, CVSS 9.8) Allows Unauthenticated Root SSH Access The Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued an alert regarding a critical authentication bypass vulnerability affecting Raisecom RAX701-GC routers used in industrial and tel ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Cache poisoning vulnerabilities found in 2 DNS resolving apps
item.content:encoded: GONNA POISON LIKE IT’S 2008 At least one CVE could weaken defenses put in place following 2008 disclosure. The makers of BIND, the Internet’s most widely used software for resolving domain names, are ... Vulnerabilities has been mentioned in this article.

item.title: Iranian hackers targeted over 100 govt orgs with Phoenix backdoor
item.author: Bill Toulas
item.content:encoded: State-sponsored Iranian hacker group MuddyWater has targeted more than 100 government entities in attacks that deployed version 4 of the Phoenix backdoor. [...]
